title: Effective phonon models based on symmetry-adapted multipole basis -- Hidden chiral phonon angular momentum splitting in ferroaxial systems
authors: Yu Xie, Rikuto Oiwa, Satoru Hayami
abstract: We propose a symmetry-based framework for constructing effective harmonic phonon models using a symmetry-adapted multipole basis. By decomposing the force-constant matrix into bond-centered electric multipoles, we identify the minimal microscopic ingredients responsible for phonon angular-momentum splitting. Applying this framework to a minimal zigzag-chain model, we show that ferroaxial order gives rise to a hidden sublattice-resolved chiral phonons, while an additional polar contribution leads to finite global chirality. Our results provide a unified symmetry-based description of hidden and emergent phonon phenomena and suggest a route to control phonon properties via electronic orderings and external fields.
summary: This paper proposes a new theoretical framework to understand how different types of crystal symmetry-breaking (ferroaxial and polar) affect phonon angular momentum. By decomposing force constants into electric multipoles, the authors show that ferroaxial systems possess a 'hidden' chirality that is only revealed as a global property when polar order is present. This provides a unified way to predict and control phonon properties through electronic ordering and external fields.
main_problem: The microscopic origin of phonon angular momentum (AM) splitting in ferroaxial systems and the mechanism by which ferroaxiality and polarity combine to create global chirality are unclear.
main_result: The authors identify that ferroaxial order creates 'hidden' sublattice-resolved chiral phonons that cancel globally, while adding polar order lifts this cancellation to produce observable global chirality.
method: A symmetry-based framework using a Symmetry-Adapted Multipole Basis (SAMB) to decompose the force-constant matrix into bond-centered electric multipoles.
model_or_system: A minimal 1D zigzag-chain model with two sublattices (A and B) in space group No. 51 (point group D2h), using a harmonic approximation for the potential energy.
key_observables: Phonon energy dispersion, phonon angular momentum (AM) distribution, and sublattice-resolved AM.
important_parameters: Electric and electric toroidal multipoles, isotropic and anisotropic force constants (k1, k2, k'1, k'2), and ferroaxial/polar order parameters.
main_assumptions: The study is restricted to the harmonic approximation (second-order expansion) and assumes a bond-based formulation where energy depends on relative displacements.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows schematics of isotropic and anisotropic interactions via multipoles; Fig 2 displays the 1D zigzag chain and its phonon dispersion; Fig 3 illustrates electric toroidal dipoles and the distribution of sublattice-resolved AM.
paper_structure: The paper introduces a new symmetry-based framework (SAMB), applies it to a 1D zigzag chain model, analyzes the emergence of hidden chirality in ferroaxial states, and demonstrates how polar contributions lead to global chirality.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

FIG. 2: (a) Schematic illustration of the one-dimensional zigzag chain. (b) Phonon energy dispersion of the model.

(c) Sublattice-resolved phonon AM distribu- tion under ferroaxial order described by ˆG(inter) z . (d) Antiferro-type chiral order of ET quadrupoles G± u cor- responding to Fig. 4(a).
